cards.back: A situation where two different input values produce the same output hash value
A process that enables two parties to securely exchange cryptographic keys over an insecure channel
A mathematical function that can be easily computed in one direction, but is computationally infeasible to invert in the other direction
A cryptographic mechanism that uses a private key to create a unique digital fingerprint, which can be verified using a corresponding public key
A cryptographic protocol that enables a party to prove the validity of a statement without revealing any additional information beyond the proof itself
